untranslatable_reason: Signal requires tracking options position state (ratio put spread with near-term/longer-term combinations, original credit received, buy-back pricing logic). These are position-level derivatives constructs not expressible through price/technical indicators alone. The core logic—'buy back near-term combination for less than original credit'—requires options Greeks, bid-ask spreads, and position P&L tracking, which are outside the scope of OHLCV-based indicators.
translation_notes: This is an options income strategy signal from McMillan's work on spreads. The entry requires an established ratio put spread position (not a market entry condition), and the exit is a position management decision based on option pricing relative to original credit received—both require options-specific data. A simplified proxy using 'close == prev_close' (stock unchanged) for entry and 'close > prev_close' (stock moved up) for exit captures the market regime intent but does not reflect the actual options buyback logic.
